headline: IDF Kills Hezbollah Intelligence Chief in Beirut Strike
summary: In direct retaliation for Hezbollah's rocket/drone salvo, IDF jets struck the Dahiyeh southern suburbs of Beirut at approximately 03:00 local time. The IDF confirmed the killing of Hussein Makled, described as 'the head of Hezbollah's intelligence headquarters,' responsible for coordinating Hezbollah's intelligence collection infrastructure. A second senior Hezbollah operative was simultaneously struck and killed in southern Lebanon.

secondary_markets.name: Elbit Systems
secondary_markets.reason: IDF precision strike confirms live-combat use of Elbit targeting and ISR systems; wartime operational tempo sustains record $25B+ order backlog and accelerates new procurement cycles.

secondary_markets.name: Lockheed Martin
secondary_markets.reason: F-35 airstrikes over Beirut demonstrate platform combat effectiveness, reinforcing allied procurement pipelines and US supplemental defense budget allocations.

secondary_markets.name: Maersk
secondary_markets.reason: Active strikes on Beirut — a key Eastern Mediterranean transshipment hub — disrupt CMA CGM and Maersk Eastern Med port call schedules, forcing costly re-routing and void sailings.

secondary_markets.name: Northrop Grumman
secondary_markets.reason: Shares jumped 6% on March 2 as stealth-bomber and missile-defense technology demand surges with the confirmed re-opening of the Lebanon front and broader Iran-theater escalation.

secondary_markets.name: War Risk Insurance Premium (Lloyd's)
secondary_markets.reason: London's Joint War Committee widened its high-risk zone following the Beirut strikes; hull war-risk premiums for the Eastern Mediterranean are spiking, directly benefiting Lloyd's syndicate underwriters.

trade_position: Long Beazley (BEZ.L) as the purest listed proxy for surging Lloyd's war-risk premium income — war-risk rates have spiked 1000%+ in the Gulf and are now spreading to the Eastern Mediterranean following the Beirut strikes, yet Beazley's equity has not fully re-rated to reflect this premium windfall. Secondary catalyst: the Joint War Committee's formal expansion of high-risk zones forces mandatory hull war-risk purchases from all transiting operators, creating a structural premium floor.

messages.text_en:
🚨BREAKING: In response to Hezbollah's rocket fire toward Israel in violation of the ceasefire agreement, the IDF has begun striking Hezbollah targets across Lebanon.

A short while ago, the IDF precisely struck senior Hezbollah terrorists in the Beirut area and a central Hezbollah terrorist in southern Lebanon.
